item.title: Defense and Publication of MSc Thesis
item.description: Last week, I had the opportunity to deliver a presentation on the research conducted during the course of my MSc thesis, titled “Exploring Unimodal, Multimodal, and Few-shot Deep Learning Methods for Diagnostic Captioning.” The audience comprised members of the thesis committee, fellow students, distinguished researchers, and faculty members. This thesis was conducted under the supervision of Prof. Ion Androutsopoulos and Prof. John Pavlopoulos. The thesis committee consisted of the first supervisor, as well as Prof. George Papaioannou and Prof. Yannis Kotidis.

item.title: Distinction in the annual ImageCLEFmedical Competition
item.description: We participated in the annual ImageCLEFmedical Caption competition, where we ranked 1st in the Concept Detection and 3rd in the Caption Prediction task. This is a distinction that our group has been systematically achieving for the last few years. You can read more here.

item.title: I have obtained a position at French CNRS
item.description: In the continuity of my MSCA fellowship, I’ve been hired by the French CNRS (Section 11) as a tenured full time researcher at LIPhy (UGA).
